reasoning:
The €STR is currently hovering steadily around 1.931%. For the shortest horizon (April 19), an ECB meeting is unlikely to have occurred, meaning any changes will be solely due to daily fluctuations (noise). Given the requirement for a *strict* increase, this probability is slightly below 50% (estimated at 46% based on recent daily empirical distribution). 

Going further into 2026, polls of economists suggest a strong asymmetry in expectations: zero or very few economists forecast rate cuts in 2026, while over a third (and some major banks) explicitly forecast rate hikes due to inflation risks exacerbated by the Middle East conflict. This skewed distribution means that conditionally, if a policy rate change occurs by late 2026, it is highly likely to be a hike. Consequently, the probability of the €STR being higher peaks around Q3/Q4 2026 at ~60%. 

Looking out to 2027 and beyond, the current macroeconomic cycle will resolve, and the ECB could reverse course depending on future, unpredictable economic conditions. In line with maximum uncertainty for ultra-long horizons, the probabilities smoothly converge back toward the base rate of 0.50.
